commands.actualOutput:
You open heavy desk.
Inside the heavy desk you see winding key.
You take winding key from heavy desk.
You push the painting aside, revealing a small wall safe hidden behind it.

You strike a match and light the logs. The fire catches quickly, casting warm light across the study.

As the flames illuminate the hearthstone, you notice numbers scratched into the soot-blackened surface: 7 - 3 - 9.
You dial 7... 3... 9. Click. The safe door swings open.
You take clock spring from wall safe.

Hallway

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

commands.actualOutput:
You insert the winding key and turn it gently. The music box comes alive -- a delicate melody fills the attic, tinkling and sweet.

As the last note fades, you hear a mechanical click from the steamer trunk -- its lock releases and the lid swings open.
Steamer trunk is already open.

You take family locket from steamer trunk.
You found your grandmother's heirloom.
You lift the locket from the trunk. Inside, your grandmother smiles back at you from a faded photograph. This is what she wanted you to have.

header.title: Naive Player Playthrough

comment.text: # A naive player looks around first
# They try to examine things they see mentioned
# A naive player tries to "ring" the doorbell — natural English
# They try to take the mat — should get a helpful redirect, not blank
# They try lifting the mat — same intent
# They see the key and take it
# A naive player might try "use key on door"
# They see the overcoat mentioned and try natural verbs
# "check pockets" is a very natural thing to try
# They see a staircase mentioned, try to climb it
# The game hints to go UP, so the naive player tries that
# They went up, now back down to continue exploring
# They see a desk and try to open it
# They see a painting and try to look behind it
# They notice the fireplace and try to light it
# They know the combo now, try to open the safe
# A naive player might just type "dig" with no target
# Or they might try "dig flower bed"
# They see the music box and try to fix it
# The trunk opened! A naive player opens it

commands.actualOutput:
You insert the winding key and turn it gently. The music box comes alive -- a delicate melody fills the attic, tinkling and sweet.

As the last note fades, you hear a mechanical click from the steamer trunk -- its lock releases and the lid swings open.
Steamer trunk is already open.

You take family locket from steamer trunk.
You found your grandmother's heirloom.
You lift the locket from the trunk. Inside, your grandmother smiles back at you from a faded photograph. This is what she wanted you to have.

comment.text: ## Front Porch — alternate verbs and nouns
# "flip" the mat — physical manipulation (parser doesn't know "flip")
# Use-based phrasing (very common from non-IF players)
## Hallway — alternate nouns and search phrasing
## Study — fire, painting, and safe puzzles
# Try opening safe before combo — should say something about needing the combo
# (Note: safe was already opened by dial, so "open safe" on already-open safe = blank — engine bug)

commands.actualOutput:
You insert the winding key and turn it gently. The music box comes alive -- a delicate melody fills the attic, tinkling and sweet.

As the last note fades, you hear a mechanical click from the steamer trunk -- its lock releases and the lid swings open.

You take family locket from steamer trunk.
You found your grandmother's heirloom.
You lift the locket from the trunk. Inside, your grandmother smiles back at you from a faded photograph. This is what she wanted you to have.
